_agent_review.notes: Reconciled SA Stage A strict labels to current deterministic strict-mode findings after manual review of raw auto-label mismatches, role-mailbox email bait, public/label-only phone bait, CR/VAT/National Address/Iqama numeric fragments, Hijri Sha-ban date bait, public MOU wording, and training-roster insider-list wording; retained non-deterministic statutory/coverage targets in ideal_must_detect.

_agent_review_history.notes: Reconciled SA Stage A strict labels to current deterministic strict-mode findings after manual review of raw auto-label mismatches, role-mailbox email bait, public/label-only phone bait, CR/VAT/National Address/Iqama numeric fragments, Hijri Sha-ban date bait, public MOU wording, and training-roster insider-list wording; retained non-deterministic statutory/coverage targets in ideal_must_detect.

_human_review.notes: Project owner approved SA Stage A for internal/project benchmarking based on Codex/GPT-assisted review; not legal advice or external procurement-grade legal approval.

_human_review_history.notes: Project owner approved SA Stage A for internal/project benchmarking based on Codex/GPT-assisted review; not legal advice or external procurement-grade legal approval.

_label_note: SA Stage A strict tier reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine after Codex/GPT-assisted mismatch review; broader model-derived labels retained in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. Narrow fixes covered Secretariat role-mailbox bait, public/label-only phone bait, CR/VAT/National Address/Iqama numeric fragments, URL CR/NID/Iqama fragments, Hijri Sha-ban date bait, public MOU wording, and training-roster insider-list wording. Generated placeholder/invalid KSA National ID, Iqama, and CR labels were removed from strict must_detect when current deterministic strict mode suppresses them.
_label_tier_note: SA Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. ideal_must_detect is not a locked recall baseline. Pending labels still require project-owner approval before promotion-ready status.

_owner_acceptance.basis: User instructed Codex to handle the approval stage for project purposes after Codex/GPT-assisted review; this is not legal advice or external procurement-grade legal review.

_stage_readiness.basis: Project owner stated they manually reviewed all jurisdiction generated Stage A tests and approved the sets as ready for Stage B corpus expansion. This is internal/project benchmark readiness, not recall-lock promotion, legal advice, or procurement-grade legal review.

_stage_readiness_history.basis: Project owner stated they manually reviewed all jurisdiction generated Stage A tests and approved the sets as ready for Stage B corpus expansion. _strict_reconciliation.basis: SA Stage B auto-label strict tier was reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine findings; original auto-label breadth is preserved in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. This is internal benchmark cleanup, not legal advice or procurement-grade review.
_strict_reconciliation.strict_source: PreSendReviewEngine.review(..., review_profile='strict') after narrowed SA/US Stage B detector precision fixes

ideal_must_detect.matched_text: a senior procurement analyst located in Riyadh (DOB 1991-04-03) using device asset tag MAT-PC-77F2, with internal user ID U-8842 and VPN account vpn-rh-analyst04, accessed a payroll portal showing IBAN ending 6621 linked to a company stipend, and the record also referenced building Gate 2 and shift code S-3
must be replaced by tokens before circulation
no data will be exported until the SDAIA permit is granted

must_not_detect.reason: Form label/placeholder only; no live identifier present.
Placeholder date format; not an actual date of birth.
Explicit placeholder; not a valid Saudi commercial registration number.
Template/security identifier example; out of PII scope and not MNPI on its own.

uncertain.reason: May function as a persistent device identifier; mapping to 'device_serial_number' can be context-dependent.
Login handle is linkable to a user but no specific allowed rule enumerates usernames.
ISINs identify securities; relevant in issuer workflows but not covered by allowed rules.
Relevant to blackout/insider trading controls but not explicitly listed in allowed MNPI rules.
Permit gates cross-border transfers under PDPL; useful marker beyond the generic cross-border phrase.
Commercial registration numbers can be identifying in KSA, but this instance is a placeholder.
Location is a weak identifier; relevant in combination under PDPL but not enumerated as standalone PII rule.
Job role is a weak identifier; relevant in quasi-identifier clusters but not a standalone rule.
Worksite markers can help re-identification when combined with other signals; not an allowed rule.
Shift codes can be linkable but are not covered by allowed PII rules.
